memberdef.argsstring: (XTft *InstancePtr, u8 CharValue, u32 ColStartVal, u32 RowStartVal, u32 FgColor, u32 BgColor)
memberdef.name: XTft_WriteChar

briefdescription.para: This function writes a particular character onto the screen using the character bitmap values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the value of the ASCII character.
is the value of the starting column. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the value of the starting row. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
is the value with which the pixels will be filled to highlight the character.
is the value with which the pixels will be filled with a different color from foreground.

simplesect.para: It must be taken care that the ASCII character which is to be written has value greater than 31 as character bitmap array starts with space as its first character.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a TFT Driver Instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

is a pointer to a XTft_Config configuration structure.This structure will contain the requested configuration for the device. Typically, this is a local structure and the content of which will be copied into the configuration structure within XTft.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the column, row position in the Instance structure of driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the column number to which ColVal member of Instance structure is updated. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the row number to which RowVal member of Instance structure is updated. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).

briefdescription.para: This function changes the column, row position in the Instance structure of driver.

detaileddescription.para: It is used to correct the position for the next character to be written if column and row position cross limits.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

is the column number to which ColVal member of Instance structure is updated. If ColVal crosses (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1) - XTFT_CHAR_WIDTH, position is moved to next line. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).

is the row number to which RowVal member of Instance structure is updated. If RowVal crosses (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1)- XTFT_CHAR_HEIGHT, the first line will be deleted. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).

simplesect.para: This function differs from the function XTft_SetPos because you cannot move to any position on the screen as we have to check for enough space for a character to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function changes the color values in the instance structure of driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the color with which member FgColor of Instance structure is updated.
is the color with which member BgColor of Instance structure is updated.

simplesect.para: Color Values must be 32bit. They can range from 0x0 to 0xFFFFFFFF, out of these only 18 bits are valid data. These 18 bits contain the Red, Green, Blue color values with 6 bits each and bit positions as Red[8-13], Green[16-21], Blue[24-29].

memberdef.argsstring: (XTft *InstancePtr, u32 ColVal, u32 RowVal, u32 PixelVal)
memberdef.name: XTft_SetPixel

briefdescription.para: This function sets the pixel with the given color at the given column, row position.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
represents the column on the screen. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
represents the row on the screen. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
represents the color with which it will be filled on screen.

simplesect.para: This API is independent of the interface the TFT Controller is connected to. The data is written to the Video Memory.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the color of the pixel at the given column, row position.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
represents the column on the screen. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
represents the row on the screen. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
stores the color value on the screen pointed by given Column and Row position.

simplesect.para: This API is independent of the interface the TFT Controller is connected to. The data is read from the Video Memory.

briefdescription.para: This function performs one of the following three operations based on the CharValue passed :

listitem.para: Move the position to the next line at the same position.
Move the position to the next line.
Write a particular character and update the column position by the width of the character.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the ASCII code value of the character to be written.

simplesect.para: As the character bitmap array does not support some of the ASCII values, to support some of those which carry significance in display are added in the switch case. If there is a necessity for any other characters, it can be added here.

briefdescription.para: This function inserts a new blank line at the bottom of the screen, it deletes the first line and moves the remaining lines up by one line.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function re-initializes all the pixels to the default background color.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (XTft *InstancePtr, u32 ColStartVal, u32 RowStartVal, u32 ColEndVal, u32 RowEndVal, u32 PixelVal)
memberdef.name: XTft_FillScreen

briefdescription.para: This function fills the screen with the range defined by the start and end values of column and row with the color passed as argument.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the value of the starting column. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the value of the starting row. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
is the value of the ending column. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the value of the ending row. The valid values are 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
represents the color with which it will be filled on screen.

simplesect.para: It must be taken care to pass values such that column start position should not exceed column end position, same is the case with row position.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the TFT display by setting the Display Enable bit in the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the TFT display by clearing the Display Enable bit in the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables reverse scan by setting the Display Scan Control(DPS) bit in the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

simplesect.para: By default the scan direction is reverse, the image we see in reverse scan mode is the horizontal and vertical mirror image of the image in normal scan mode.

briefdescription.para: This function enables normal scan by clearing the Display Scan Control(DPS) bit in the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function changes the Video Memory Base address stored in the Address Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the new memory address value to be written to the Address register.

simplesect.para: The new memory address must be in the address range of external memory and it must be taken care that 2MB memory is available from this new address so that frame data to 640*480 screen can be written.

briefdescription.para: Write a value to a TFT register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the register offset from the base to write to.
is the data written to the register.

briefdescription.para: Read a value from a TFT register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the register offset from the base to read from.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the Vsync Interrupt in the Interrupt Enable and Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the Vsync Interrupt in the Interrupt Enable and Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Vsync(Video address latch) status from the Interrupt Enable and Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if Vsync pulse has occurred after displaying the current frame.
FALSE if the TFT core has not completed displaying the current frame.

simplesect.para: Vsync(Video address latch) Status bit will be cleared by the HW after it loads the address from the Address Register and starts displaying the Frame.
